mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate determines the risk-free discount rate; a higher level compresses the present value of future earnings and drives negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of top-quartile FEDFUNDS observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% signals deteriorating household income and collapsing consumption demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and causes negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are non-negative in at least 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high CPIAUCSL and high FEDFUNDS generate stagflationary pressure that erodes corporate profit margins and drives negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with elevated inflation
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS carries a positive coefficient on 60-day returns over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction fails to raise adjusted R-squared by more than 2 percentage points versus additive specifications.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals rising capacity utilization and revenue expansion, but this positive transmission to equities is conditional on an accommodative monetary environment where FEDFUNDS is below 4.0%, producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 4.0% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales reflects weakening consumer spending, which directly lowers expected corporate revenues and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month RSAFS changes are followed by positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial production indicates increasing capacity utilization and operating leverage, which amplifies earnings growth and generates positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive 3-month changes in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of non-recessionary quarters over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter contraction in real GDP signals recessionary demand destruction and falling corporate profits, which drives negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly contractions in GDPC1 are followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1800 thousand units signal robust construction activity and positive household wealth effects, which lift aggregate demand and produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: HOUST readings above 1800 are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.
